stories.headline: Trump uses Mount Rushmore speech to warn of ‘communist menace’ in US
stories.summary: President kicked off 250th anniversary events with a preview of a likely line of attack against Democrats in coming elections

stories.headline: Trump praises army, rails against communism in US 250th anniversary speech
stories.summary: At Mount Rushmore Trump warns of 'communist menace', ties rhetoric to immigration ahead of November midterms.

stories.headline: Oil Terminal in Russia’s St. Petersburg Comes Under Attack
stories.summary: Kyiv’s forces attacked an oil terminal in St. Petersburg, Russia’s second largest city, with drones overnight, local officials said, while Ukraine said a gas production facility was struck by Russia.

stories.headline: In pictures: Stars and fans celebrate Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding
stories.summary: Celebrities and sports stars were spotted as the A-list couple tied the knot in New York on Friday.

stories.headline: Energy-Hungry Asia Is Already Drawing Lessons From Iran Crisis
stories.summary: There is still no lasting peace deal in the Persian Gulf, but energy-hungry Asia is already drawing energy lessons from four months of war: it needs bigger buffers, a greater diversity of fossil-fuel suppliers, and a better mix of power sources overall.

stories.headline: At Mount Rushmore, Trump Veers From Patriotism to ‘Communism’
stories.summary: On the eve of July 4, President Trump extolled the nation’s founders while branding his opponents as “communists” in what seemed to be a warm-up for November.

stories.headline: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce marry at NYC’s Madison Square Garden
stories.summary: A-list celebrities attend wedding at Madison Square Garden to see superstar couple tie the knot.

stories.headline: ‘El Obeid crisis could be worse than El Fasher,’ warns ex-UN official
stories.summary: Mukesh Kapila, ex-UN Humanitarian Coordinator for Sudan warns that, 'El Obeid crisis could be worse than El Fasher'.

stories.headline: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
stories.summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

stories.headline: Large crowds gather in Tehran on first day of Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral
stories.summary: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla until Monday, before travelling across Iran and Iraq.

stories.headline: 'Flamingo Revolution' takes off as thousands demand Albanian PM's resignation
stories.summary: Flamingos have become a national symbol of protest, triggered by opposition to a luxury development.

stories.summary: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla from Friday ahead of days-long funeral events.

stories.headline: Stephen Breyer: Can we keep this great experiment?
stories.summary: There are some who fear not, but I am optimistic. The students I meet are interested in defending their Constitution

stories.headline: Yankee pyramids: how to build a presidential library
stories.summary: Every modern US president gets a physical totem of his tenure. What can they teach us about America’s struggle to remember itself?

stories.headline: Macron’s Budget Finale Augurs More Political Chaos in France
stories.summary: The final budget showdown of Emmanuel Macron’s presidency is already gearing up to be France’s highest-stakes fiscal clash of the decade so far.

stories.headline: China Coast Guard Rotates Patrol Task Group East of Taiwan
stories.summary: China’s coast guard said a task group led by the CCGS Xiushan vessel replaced the team headed by CCGS Daishan on Saturday to continue law-enforcement patrols in waters east of Taiwan.

stories.headline: A Silk Road destination wants tourists to stay longer
stories.summary: Tourist trips to Uzbekistan rose from 6.63 million in 2023 to 11.7 million in 2025. Authorities are linking that growth to new air routes, Central Asian travel packages and higher tourism revenue.

stories.headline: Polish PM warns critical months ahead in face of Russian threat
stories.summary: Tusk says Poland is preparing for "various" scenarios after media reports of a planned Russian attack.

stories.headline: At least 32 dead after overcrowded bus plunges into ravine in Pakistan
stories.summary: The bus was travelling from Quetta to Peshawar, when it crashed in the Dana Sar mountain range.

stories.headline: Monaco bomb suspect is a woman from Ukraine

stories.headline: Pilot Was Bitten by Bat After Swarm Invaded Hotel Room, Lawsuit Says
stories.summary: A pilot, who was treated for possible rabies exposure, sued Marriott International, claiming his Denver hotel failed to offer him a new room after he woke up to a swarm of bats last year.

stories.headline: At the L.A. Warehouse Fire, First It Was the Smoke. Now It’s the Smell.
stories.summary: Rotting food — millions of pounds of it — remains in an L.A. cold-storage facility damaged in a fire. Residents say the cleanup hasn’t been fast enough.

stories.headline: ‘I can only describe it as a war zone’: the rescuers navigating Venezuela’s post-quake hellscape

stories.headline: Quakes reduce Venezuela tourist town to rubble, leaving economy in tatters
stories.summary: Quakes reduce Venezuela tourist town to rubble, leaving economy in tatters.

stories.headline: Conservative candidate Keiko Fujimori wins Peru's presidential election in runoff
stories.summary: Voters were primarily concerned with increasing levels of crime, especially extortion by violent organized crime gangs, and Fujimori pledged to combat crime with an iron fist.

stories.headline: Bomb blast at Damascus cafe kills nine, Syrian state media say
stories.summary: There was no immediate claim for the bombing at a cafe close to the Palace of Justice, a major government building.

stories.headline: Trump will oppose heavy US AI regulation, says outgoing tech adviser

stories.headline: Venture Capital During the AI Revolution: Masters in Business with Mamoon Hamid
stories.summary: Barry sits down with Kleiner Perkins Partner, Mamoon Hamid. They discuss Mamoon's thoughts on the AI revolution and his approach to early AI investing.  Mamoon also breaks down how he became an early investor in giants like Slack, Figma, and how Kleiner Perkins assesses the investments they missed. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Toronto Stocks Enjoy Broad Gains After New Pipeline Proposal
stories.summary: Canada’s stocks benchmark climbed Friday morning after Prime Minister Mark Carney unveiled a new pipeline project to the country’s West Coast.

stories.headline: Copper Tracks Dollar Swings as Traders Weigh US Rate Outlook
stories.summary: Copper rose on Friday, tracking small swings in the US dollar as investors continue to assess the outlook for US monetary policy.

stories.headline: Gold Heads for First Weekly Gain Since May on Easing Fed Outlook
stories.summary: Gold headed for its first weekly gain since May as traders dialed down their expectations of Federal Reserve rate hikes.

stories.headline: ECB’s Lagarde Will Attend Next Week’s Ecofin Instead of Vujcic
stories.summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde will be attending next week’s meeting of European Union finance ministers in Brussels instead of her vice president.

stories.headline: European Stocks at Record, Book Fourth Straight Week of Gains
stories.summary: European stocks advanced on Friday, closing at a record high and recording a fourth straight week of gains as investors remained optimistic that the Federal Reserve will hold off on rate hikes for now.

stories.headline: Argentina Pushes $6 Billion in Repo Maturities Past Election
stories.summary: Argentina’s central bank extended maturities worth $6 billion on its repurchase agreements — or repos — to ease the government debt burden going into the 2027 presidential election.

stories.headline: Yen Strengthens as Traders Brace for Holiday Intervention Risk
stories.summary: Options traders are paying up to hedge against sharp moves in the yen ahead of thin US holiday trading, as speculation grows that Japanese authorities may be less predictable in how they intervene to support the currency.

stories.headline: OPEC Output Surged in June as Hormuz Flows Jumped, Survey Shows
stories.summary: OPEC’s crude oil production surged last month as Persian Gulf members restored exports through the Strait of Hormuz amid a peace accord between the US and Iran, according to a Bloomberg survey.

stories.headline: 'Two weeks after her death I got a call': Gaza patients face agonising delays for evacuation
stories.summary: An estimated 300 Palestinians referred for treatment abroad have died since the ceasefire began, according to Gaza's Hamas-run health ministry.

stories.headline: Watch: BBC at site of deadly Russian attack on Kyiv flats

stories.headline: China says pilot crashed small plane into skyscraper for 'personal reasons'
stories.summary: The 66-year-old, who died in the crash, had anxiety and referenced "ending his life" in his diary.

stories.headline: Aunt of Venezuelan boy pulled from rubble tells BBC she will give him 'mother's warmth'

stories.headline: Oil Is Little Changed as Persian Gulf Flows Near Pre-War Levels

stories.headline: Did this AI anti-drug video make drugs look appealing?
stories.summary: Hong Kong’s Correctional Services Department removed an AI-generated K-pop video, after facing backlash that it made substances look too appealing.

stories.headline: The US deported them to Venezuela - hours later earthquakes struck

stories.headline: Why the expected fight over the North American trade deal never kicked off

stories.headline: Canada, Philippines Agree to Sign Trade Deal This Year, Grow Defense Ties
stories.summary: Canada and the Philippines plan to finalize a free trade agreement this year, and signed agreements to deepen cooperation on energy, defense, labor and migration, Prime Minister Mark Carney said Thursday.

stories.headline: European Stocks Rally to Cap Best Week Since May: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Stocks rose as the latest round of jitters about the artificial-intelligence trade subsided, with Europe’s benchmark rising to an all-time high. The dollar touched a two-week low while gold extended gains.

stories.headline: Can China target critics abroad with its new 'ethnic unity' law?

stories.headline: Budapest's first Pride since Orban left power

stories.headline: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline
stories.summary: Very few North Koreans know about her, as her origin could threaten the regime’s legitimacy.
